title: Entity grounding/normalization to HGNC/MONDO/Reactome/ChEMBL/SO (precision-first)
context: Entities must be grounded to standard IDs (precision over recall) so the graph reconciles against the wider biomedical linked-data web; ChEMBL (CC BY-SA) crosswalks are segregated/labeled. Only open / public-domain / openly-licensed sources (CIViC CC0, Open Targets CC0, Reactome, license-checked PMC open-access subset); COSMIC, OncoKB, DrugBank, any non-open DB and all controlled-access / patient-level data (dbGaP, EGA, biobanks, EHR) are out of scope and refused. No patient data; all therapeutic-target content is research evidence labeled 'not medical advice'. See PLAN.md.

acceptanceCriteria: Entities grounded to standard IDs (geneâ†’HGNC/Ensembl, diseaseâ†’MONDO, pathwayâ†’Reactome, compoundâ†’ChEMBL, sequence featureâ†’SO); â‰¥95% grounding coverage.
Uses an explicit candidate-blocking strategy (normalized symbol + entity type + context); the grounder is tuned for precision over recall.
Zero confirmed false mappings in the grounding audit sample.
output: A grounding/normalization crosswalk dataset (entityâ†’standard ID) with audit, committed via PR.
